diagrams.title: Binary Search Process on Array [2,5,8,12,16,23,38,56,72,91]
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of searching for target=23 in a sorted array
diagrams.purpose: Shows how search space halves with each comparison, requiring only 3 steps vs 10 for linear search

diagrams.title: Decision Tree for Binary Search (n=8)
diagrams.description: Binary tree showing all possible decision paths when n=8
elements: Root: compare with element at index 3
Level 1: if less compare index 1, if greater compare index 5
Level 2: leaf nodes represent individual elements
Depth = log2(8) = 3, meaning maximum 3 comparisons needed
Shows balanced nature of Binary Search decisions
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates why time complexity is O(log n) - tree depth grows logarithmically with array size

diagrams.title: Iterative vs Recursive Binary Search Stack
diagrams.description: Comparison of memory usage between iterative and recursive implementations
elements: Iterative: single stack frame with low, high, mid variables (O(1) space)
Recursive: multiple stack frames - each call adds one frame (O(log n) frames)
Shows call stack buildup and unwinding for recursive version
Highlights potential stack overflow risk for recursive with large arrays
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates space complexity difference between the two implementation approaches
